fields.description:
## Why

Agents refining review criteria repeatedly blur the four-pass architecture by adding severity,
drop/block policy, or remediation instructions to Pass 1. Similar drift can make Pass 2 discover
new issues or prescribe fixes, make Pass 3 depend on model judgment, or make Pass 4 re-adjudicate
findings. Those changes erode the deliberate tension between a critical finder and a generous,
independent verifier.

## What

Dogfood rebar's project extension surface by authoring one code-review criterion,
`project.review-phase-boundaries`, with four co-located subrules:

- **Pass 1 — find:** discover grounded candidate findings only; never assess validity, impact,
  severity, confidence, drop/block posture, or remediation.
- **Pass 2 — verify:** independently answer atomic validity and impact questions only; never
  discover new findings, decide outcomes, or prescribe fixes.
- **Pass 3 — decide:** apply deterministic thresholds and partition findings without an LLM; never
  gather new evidence or coach.
- **Pass 4 — coach:** offer non-prescriptive next-move guidance for already-surfaced findings;
  never re-verify, alter the decision, or dictate an exact implementation.

The criterion itself is a Pass-1 finder: it emits grounded evidence only, carries no
severity/confidence, and always leaves `suggested_fix` empty.

## Scope

In scope:

- A committed project prompt at
  `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md`, routing/activation entry, and
  balanced eval spec at
  `.rebar/evals/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml`.
- Routing with `block_threshold: 0.90` and `blocking_enabled: false`; the threshold is recorded for
  pilot analysis but cannot block during this story.
- Use routing `exec: "1-TURN"` and prompt `execution_mode: single_turn`. The finder receives the
  assembled diff, needs no repository tools or iterative calls, and returns one structured finding
  payload; single-turn keeps the pilot's runtime/cost proportional to one Pass-1 call.
- Guards that distinguish operative instructions from documentation or negative examples, require
  identifiable phase ownership, and abstain when ownership is unclear.
- Grandfather existing `suggested_fix` fields: unchanged debt is not a finding. Newly adding or
  expanding remediation instructions in Pass 1 is a finding. Removal of the field/contract is a
  separate cleanup when those core contracts are next modified.
- A balanced twelve-case corpus: `RP-F1..RP-F6` must fire and `RP-N1..RP-N6` must not fire.
- Record the live three-run eval on this ticket with model, UTC timestamp, all 36 executions, and
  the aggregate/stability metrics required below.
- Add a `Project dogfood: review-phase-boundaries` subsection to `docs/llm-framework.md` explaining
  the project-owned placement, phase contract, advisory posture, and eval corpus.

Out of scope: the generic integration seam (dependency bug), live Gerrit rollout, changing the
kernel's impact model, removing grandfathered `suggested_fix`, or enabling blocking.

## Eval Contract

Each YAML case uses the `diff` key. `criteria eval` resolves the sanitized physical prompt id and
reuses eval_solver's existing `_run_code_review_case` per-prompt arm, which supplies the diff as
`ticket_context` and scores the native `{findings: [...]}` output. This calibrates the project
Pass-1 finder directly; it does not invoke plan-review `pass1_chunk` or run the full four-pass gate.

Must-fire cases cover: Pass-1 validity/impact grading; Pass-1 drop/block policy; Pass-1 remediation;
Pass-2 new-finding/decision/fix leakage; an LLM/discretionary Pass 3; and Pass-4 re-adjudication or
exact implementation prescription.

Must-not-fire cases cover: refining Pass-1 discovery scope; strengthening Pass-1 evidence rules;
refining Pass-2 atomic binary questions; changing deterministic Pass-3 threshold configuration;
adding non-prescriptive Pass-4 next-move coaching; and documenting a forbidden pattern or unchanged
grandfathered debt.

The corpus pins these concrete instructions:

- `RP-F1`: Pass 1 assigns candidate validity and impact; expect a Pass-1 assessment-leak finding.
- `RP-F2`: Pass 1 drops low-priority candidates or marks them blocking; expect a Pass-1 policy-leak
  finding.
- `RP-F3`: Pass 1 supplies an exact replacement/patch; expect a Pass-1 remediation-leak finding.
- `RP-F4`: Pass 2 may invent an additional issue, choose disposition, or direct a fix; expect a
  Pass-2 ownership finding.
- `RP-F5`: an LLM weighs evidence or chooses disposition in Pass 3; expect a deterministic-policy
  finding.
- `RP-F6`: Pass 4 overturns a finding or dictates an exact implementation; expect a coaching
  boundary finding.
- `RP-N1`: Pass 1 narrows the problem class it discovers; expect no finding.
- `RP-N2`: Pass 1 strengthens file/line/quote evidence requirements; expect no finding.
- `RP-N3`: Pass 2 asks atomic validity and impact yes/no questions; expect no finding.
- `RP-N4`: deterministic Pass-3 routing changes a numeric threshold or advisory posture; expect no
  finding.
- `RP-N5`: Pass 4 offers an investigative next move without selecting the implementation; expect no
  finding.
- `RP-N6`: documentation/eval text quotes a forbidden pattern, or a diff shows only unchanged
  grandfathered `suggested_fix` context; expect no finding.

Only added or modified normative instructions in prompt, workflow-op, or criterion content are
operative. Documentation, tests, eval expectations, explicitly labeled examples, and unchanged diff
context are descriptive. A candidate must identify the owning phase from the changed instruction;
otherwise the criterion abstains.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` activates `project.review-phase-boundaries` for `code_review`
  with `exec: "1-TURN"`, `block_threshold: 0.90`, and `blocking_enabled: false`; verify with
  `.venv/bin/python -c 'import json; d=json.load(open(".rebar/criteria_routing.json")); r=d["code_review"]["project.review-phase-boundaries"]; assert "project.review-phase-boundaries" in d["activate"] and r["exec"] == "1-TURN" and r["block_threshold"] == 0.90 and r["blocking_enabled"] is False'`.
- [ ] `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md` encodes all four
  allowed/prohibited boundaries plus the
  instruction-vs-description, unclear-phase abstention, negative-example, and grandfathered-debt
  guards and declares `execution_mode: single_turn`. A runner-backed test inspects emitted findings
  and asserts grounded evidence is present, `severity`/`confidence` are absent, and
  `suggested_fix` is absent or empty; verify with
  `.venv/bin/python -c 'from pathlib import Path; s=Path(".rebar/prompts/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md").read_text(); assert "execution_mode: single_turn" in s' &&
  .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_review_phase_boundaries.py`.
- [ ] The criterion emits one evidence-grounded finding for each `RP-F1..RP-F6`, emits none for
  `RP-N1..RP-N6`, never emits severity/confidence, and leaves `suggested_fix` empty. Across three
  runs, recall is `1.0`, false-accept is `0.0`, agreement is `1.0`, minimum per-case stability is at
  least `2/3`, and Cohen kappa is at least `0.70`; verify the committed corpus with
  `.venv/bin/rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json |
  .venv/bin/python -c 'import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert d["recall"] == 1.0 and
  d["false_accept"] == 0.0 and d["agreement"] == 1.0 and d["stability_min"] >= 2/3 and
  d["kappa"] >= 0.70, d'`.
- [ ] [operator-attested] A complete three-run live eval is recorded on this ticket with model,
  timestamp, all 36 case executions, recall `1.0`, false-accept `0.0`, agreement `1.0`, minimum
  per-case stability at least `2/3`, and Cohen kappa at least `0.70`.
- [ ] The criterion is entirely project-owned: its implementation diff changes only committed
  `.rebar/` prompt/routing/eval assets, their `.gitignore` exceptions, criterion-specific tests,
  and the `Project dogfood: review-phase-boundaries` subsection of `docs/llm-framework.md`; verify
  with
  `.venv/bin/python -c 'import subprocess; allowed={".rebar/criteria_routing.json", ".rebar/prompts/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md", ".rebar/evals/code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml", ".gitignore", "tests/unit/test_project_review_phase_boundaries.py", "docs/llm-framework.md"}; commits=subprocess.check_output(["git", "log", "origin/main", "--format=%H", "--grep=rebar-ticket: 225a-323c-a9f6-436b"], text=True).split(); assert commits, "criterion commit is not on origin/main"; changed={p for commit in commits for p in subprocess.check_output(["git", "show", "--format=", "--name-only", commit], text=True).splitlines() if p}; extra=changed-allowed; assert not extra, f"non-project paths changed: {sorted(extra)}"' &&
  .venv/bin/python -c 'from pathlib import Path; s=Path("docs/llm-framework.md").read_text(); assert "Project dogfood: review-phase-boundaries" in s' &&
  .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_review_phase_boundaries.py && make lint`.
- [ ] The criterion change is landed on `main`; verify with
  `git log origin/main --oneline --grep='rebar-ticket: 225a-323c-a9f6-436b'`.

## Dependency

Depends on the linked integration-seam story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02`, whose child bug
`8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` owns the concrete repair. This story must not add a core special case to bypass
that seam.
